Patch Set 10: Code-Review+1

(4 comments)

while we're here can we extend the protocol so that we caller can specify how to proceed in case of error: stop processing and return an error or keep processing and collect all the errors? I'd like to use something like this to batch destroys. while this is possible to rebuild another batch in case of error, I think it's easier to just support both modes.

Patch Set 18: Code-Review+1

I'm suggesting to file a ticket to fix that issue properly. we shouldn't start transaction for non-modifying updates, but we also should stop transaction once such an update (or how we'd name it) is met. it doesn't really make sense to hold a transaction open doing some I/O for unrelated update.

there are two options here: either use batchid=0 for non-modifying updates and stop transaction once batchid changes or explicitly stop transaction when non-modifying update is met (by opcode essentially).

Di, there haven't been any changes to this patch based on the past three inspections from Alex and John. If the patch is ready to land but there are a few minor fixups then it is ok not to refresh it, but if the patch is being refreshed then it makes sense to fix up the issues found and avoid leaving them in the code for so wine else to clean up in the future.

Andreas, sorry, I miss the comments. Thanks

Only start transaction, if the update will modify the
filesystem.
